name: Guadalquivir Riverbank Trail
name_original: De Marmolejo a la Ribera del Guadalquivir — circular
description: This moderate 5.7-mile circular jogging route near Marmolejo follows the Ribera del Guadalquivir, passing the historic Balneario de Marmolej
region_intro: Jogging routes around Marmolejo are set within the province of Jaén, Spain, at the foot of the Sierra Morena Mountains and bordering the Sierras de Andújar Natural Park. The region is characterized by Mediterranean forests, riverine paths along the Guadalquivir River, and varied terrain suitable for running. This geographical setting provides a mix of flat riverbank trails and more undulating paths within the natural park. Best jogging routes around Marmolejo The most popular jogging route is Guadalquivir Riverbank Trail, a 5.6 miles (9.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 2 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the banks of the Guadalquivir River. Another top favourite among local runners is Balneario de Marmolejo - circular trail running along the Guadalquivir river bank, a moderate 6.0 miles (9.7 km) path. This route offers scenic views along the river. Local runners also love the Marmolejo – Marmolejo Spa loop from Marmolejo, a 11.8 miles (19.0 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 13 minutes. Jogging around Marmolejo is defined by river paths, Mediterranean forests, and trails within the Sierra Morena foothills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside runs to more challenging routes with elevation changes. The routes in Marmolejo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 1.7 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 270 runners have used komoot to explore Marmolejo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Marmolejo?
A: There are over 10 dedicated running routes around Marmolejo, offering a variety of distances and terrains for you to explore. These routes are set within the beautiful landscapes of Jaén, Spain.

Q: Are there running routes for all skill levels in Marmolejo?
A: Yes, Marmolejo offers a range of running routes suitable for different abilities. You'll find easy paths, such as those along the Guadalquivir River, as well as more challenging routes with elevation changes in the Sierra Morena foothills. For instance, there is 1 easy route, 8 moderate routes, and 2 difficult routes available.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Marmolejo?
A: Absolutely! Many runners prefer circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989208096/balneario-de-marmolejo-circular-trail-running-along-the-guadalquivir-river-bank" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balneario de Marmolejo - circular trail running along the Guadalquivir river bank&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 9.7 km path offering scenic views. Another longer circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marmolejo – Marmolejo Spa loop from Marmolejo&lt;/a&gt;, covering 19.0 km.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the running trails in Marmolejo?
A: The running trails in Marmolejo offer diverse and picturesque scenery. You can expect to run along the tranquil banks of the Guadalquivir River, through Mediterranean forests, and within the foothills of the Sierra Morena Mountains. The region borders the Sierras de Andújar Natural Park, providing a rich natural backdrop.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or natural features to see along the running routes?
A: Yes, several routes pass by or are near notable landmarks and natural features. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5911606" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Martín Gonzalo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, a picturesque natural environment, or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marmolejo Spa&lt;/a&gt;. Some trails also offer views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5061639" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valley Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Marmolejo?
A: The running routes in Marmolejo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 1.7 stars. Runners often praise the varied terrain, from flat riverbank paths to more undulating trails, and the beautiful natural settings, especially along the Guadalquivir River and within the natural park surroundings.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed jog?
A: Yes, if you're looking for an easier run, the region offers options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123417" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marmolejo loop from Marmolejo&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 5.2 km route with minimal elevation change. The riverbank paths generally provide a gentler experience.

Q: What is the longest running route available in Marmolejo?
A: For those seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marmolejo – Marmolejo Spa loop from Marmolejo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 19.0 km trail. It leads through varied landscapes and typically takes over 2 hours to complete.

Q: Are the running trails in Marmolejo dog-friendly?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on every trail can vary, generally, trails in natural parks and along riverbanks in Spain are dog-friendly, especially if dogs are kept on a leash. It's always a good idea to check local signage or park rules for the specific area you plan to run in, particularly within the Sierras de Andújar Natural Park.

Q: Is Marmolejo suitable for family-friendly running or walks?
A: Yes, Marmolejo offers routes that can be enjoyed by families. The flatter sections along the Guadalquivir River, such as parts of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305179286/guadalquivir-riverbank-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalquivir Riverbank Trail&lt;/a&gt;, are generally suitable for families looking for a relaxed jog or walk. These areas often include picnic spots and recreational facilities.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Marmolejo?
A: Marmolejo, located in Jaén, Spain, experiences hot summers. Therefore, the best times for running are typically during the cooler months of &lt;strong&gt;spring&lt;/strong&gt; (March to May) and &lt;strong&gt;autumn&lt;/strong&gt; (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is more temperate, making outdoor activities more enjoyable. Early mornings or late afternoons are also recommended during warmer periods.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer a good workout with significant elevation gain?
A: Yes, for a more challenging workout, consider routes that venture into the Sierra Morena foothills. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38173451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marmolejo Spa loop from Balneario de Marmolejo (museo)&lt;/a&gt;, for example, features an elevation gain of nearly 300 meters over its 13.9 km distance, providing a good test of endurance.
tips: This municipality in the Campiña de Jaén enjoys a privileged location, as it is located between Sierra Morena, the Guadalquivir fertile plain and the Sierra de Andújar natural park. This facilitates the diversity of ecosystems. This town is also famous for its medicinal waters.

The Spa of Marmolejo is located along the Guadalquivir river situated at the foot of the Sierra Morena mountains. Popular tourist attraction. From the bridge of the A-420 highway you can see the glass dome of the spa and the river.

faqs.question: How long does it take to jog this circular route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is designed for jogging and typically takes about 1 hour and 2 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features easily accessible paths. You'll encounter a mix of paved and unpaved sections, offering a varied jogging experience along the route.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy picturesque views along the Guadalquivir River. A significant highlight is the historic Marmolejo Spa, known for its distinctive glass dome. The route also offers opportunities to observe riverside birds and the diverse landscapes surrounding Marmolejo.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While the trail is rated as moderate for jogging and requires a good level of physical fitness, its easily accessible paths make it suitable for a range of abilities. Beginners with some jogging experience should find it manageable.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally dog-friendly. We recommend keeping your dog on a leash to ensure their safety and respect for other trail users and local wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit, nor are there any entrance fees to access this circular jogging route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to jog this route. The weather is milder, and the natural scenery, including riverside birds, is particularly vibrant. Summers can be very hot in this region.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Marmolejo. You can typically find parking within the town, especially near the Balneario de Marmolejo, which is a key landmark on the route.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that are part of the Sendero de Sierra Morena, Transandalus, Ribera Guadalquivir, and VP Cordel de Recoche, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants nearby?
faqs.answer: As the trail starts and ends in Marmolejo, you'll find various cafes, restaurants, and other amenities within the town to refuel before or after your jog.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a jog on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, we recommend bringing appropriate jogging shoes, water, sun protection (especially during warmer months), and a charged phone for navigation or emergencies.
